name: Varunprasaath R. Selvaraju, Ph.D.
summary: PhD chemist/materials scientist with 6+ years in physicochemical characterization, method documentation, instrument troubleshooting, and analytical structure-property studies. Experienced with UV-Vis/FT-IR, GPC/SEC, NMR, GC-MS/LC-MS exposure, TGA/DSC, DOE/SPC, and technical reporting that support material-quality decisions and assay/method transfer readiness.

education.details:
Dissertation: Modular Design and Electrochemical Properties of Xanthene-Based Electrochromic Materials
Advisor: Prof. Seth Marder
(Transferred from Georgia Institute of Technology)

education.degree: B.S. (Research), Major: Chemistry; Minor: Materials Science
education.details: INSPIRE Scholarship for Higher Education (Top 1% nationally)

bullets: Lead DOE-guided process-development and analytical readout design for bio-based polymer systems, defining reproducibility criteria, process windows, and SOP-ready specifications.
Translate FT-IR, thermal-analysis, spectroscopy, and process data into technical recommendations and documented methods for repeatable execution.
Designed, purified, and characterized 15+ organic material variants using UV-Vis-NIR, FT-IR, cyclic voltammetry, spectroelectrochemistry, NMR, GPC/SEC, TGA, DSC, and MS-informed analysis.
Built structured datasets and written summaries connecting chemical structure, purity, stability, and analytical signals to material performance and troubleshooting decisions.
Co-led thin-film coating process development, linking CVD/surface-preparation variables to morphology, interface quality, optical performance, and reproducible process controls.
Optimized polymer thin-film deposition using DOE strategies and in-situ QCM-D process monitoring to quantify growth kinetics and coating reproducibility.

Managed characterization/process-development lab operations for a 15-member group; implemented equipment tracking, calibration routines, SOPs, and safety protocols that improved instrument uptime by 30% and strengthened data integrity.

projects.title: Physicochemical Method Development Bridge
projects.description: Connects material-quality questions to LC-MS/SEC/UV-Vis/FT-IR-style analytical readouts, documentation, validation mindset, and troubleshooting.
projects.tech: LC-MS exposure, GPC/SEC, UV-Vis, FT-IR, method documentation

projects.title: Analytical Stability and Degradation Study
projects.description: Mapped degradation and performance failure modes across functional material variants using orthogonal analytical characterization.
projects.tech: UV-Vis-NIR, FT-IR, CV, TGA/DSC, NMR, GPC, MS-informed analysis

projects.title: Lab Operations and Instrument Reliability
projects.description: Managed SOPs, calibration, safety, training, and equipment systems supporting dependable multi-user characterization workflows.
projects.tech: Instrument uptime, SOPs, troubleshooting, data integrity

publications: Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Venta, R.; Rajapaksha, I.; Zhang, J.; Barlow, S.; Salman, S.; Scott, C.; Marder, S. "Narrow-Band Electrochromism of a Ferrocene-Substituted Rhodamine B." Chemistry of Materials, 2025, 37, 5558–5568.

Mandal, J.; Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Yan, W.; Divandari, M.; Spencer, N. D.; Dubner, M. "In Situ Monitoring of SI-ATRP Throughout Multiple Reinitiations Under Flow by Means of a Quartz Crystal Microbalance." RSC Advances, 2018, 8, 20048–20055.

Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Venta, R.; Rajapaksha, I.; Zhang, J.; Barlow, S.; Salman, S.; Scott, C.; Marder, S. "Beyond Rhodamine B: Investigating Alternative Xanthene Cores for Modular Electrochromic Systems." (Manuscript under preparation).
conferences: Modular Design and Electrochemical Properties of Xanthene-Based Electrochromic Materials, ACS Fall National Meeting, Denver, CO, August 2024.
Electrochemical and Spectroscopic Properties of Substituted Rhodamine Derivatives, Gerischer-Lewerenz Electrochemistry Symposium, Fort Collins, CO, August 2024.
Decoupling Redox and Color for High-Contrast Electrochromics, Excited State Processes Conference, Santa Fe, NM, June 2023.
Electrochromism: Decoupling Color and Redox in Rhodamine-Based Systems, Rocky Mountain Solid State Chemistry Workshop, Boulder, CO, January 2023.

skills.Method Development & Validation Mindset: Reproducibility studies, assay/method documentation, SOPs, data summaries, troubleshooting, transfer readiness, validation/qualification language
skills.Materials & Chemistry: Organic/materials chemistry, polymers, protein-reagent/bioconjugation concepts to be bridged, thin films, structure-property analysis
skills.Data & Communication: DOE, SPC, Excel, Python/basic, JMP/basic, technical reports, publications, cross-functional presentations, lab notebooks

honors: Plastic Sustainability Innovation in Material Award
